# Pipeline name - shows in GitHub Actions tab
name: Build, Test and Deploy to Azure

# TRIGGERS - when does this pipeline run?
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master        # Run when code pushed to master
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - master        # Run when PR created against master

# JOBS - what work gets done?
jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: windows-latest   # Use Windows server to match our app

    steps:
      # Step 1 - Download your code from GitHub onto the build server
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Step 2 - Install .NET 9 on the build server
      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '9.0.x'

      # Step 3 - Download NuGet packages (EF Core, Dapper, Swashbuckle etc)
      - name: Restore dependencies
        run: dotnet restore

      # Step 4 - Compile the code in Release mode
      # Release mode = optimized for production, no debug symbols
      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build --no-restore --configuration Release

      # Step 5 - Run all 6 unit tests
      # If any test fails, pipeline stops here - code never deploys!
      - name: Test
        run: dotnet test --no-build --configuration Release --verbosity normal

      # Step 6 - Package the API ready for deployment
      # Creates optimized production files in ./publish folder
      - name: Publish
        run: dotnet publish SmartJobTracker.API/SmartJobTracker.API.csproj --configuration Release --output ./publish

      # Step 7 - Upload packaged files to Azure App Service
      # Uses the secret we saved in GitHub to authenticate with Azure
      - name: Deploy to Azure
        uses: azure/webapps-deploy@v2
        with:
          app-name: 'smartjobtracker-api'
          publish-profile: ${{ secrets.AZURE_WEBAPP_PUBLISH_PROFILE }}
          package: ./publish
